channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Traffic Diversions Implemented in Islamabad’s Red Zone Due to Planned Protests
item.description: Islamabad authorities have closed multiple routes leading into the city’s Red Zone on Friday ahead of planned demonstrations by religious parties. The police confirmed that Margalla Road and the route...

item.title: The Decline of Western Influence in the Middle East and the Risks of Escalation
item.description: The article analyzes the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, emphasizing Iran's resilience against Western and Israeli interventions. The author argues that Iran will not collapse like Iraq, Syria, o...

item.title: Pete Edochie Details His High Daily Electricity Expenses Amid Nigeria’s Power Crisis
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actor, Pete Edochie, don talk how e dey spend huge money just to keep light for him house. For recent interview on AIT, the 78-year-old actor reveal say e dey spend about ₦40,000 eve...

item.title: Nitish Kumar moves to Rajya Sabha, JD(U) faces uncertain future in Bihar
item.description: Nitish Kumar, a prominent political figure in Bihar and disciple of Jayaprakash Narayan, has filed his nomination for the Rajya Sabha following a decisive performance in the recent Bihar assembly elec...

item.title: Jets secure 4-1 victory over Lightning ahead of trade deadline
item.description: On March 5, 2026, the Winnipeg Jets defeated the Tampa Bay Lightning 4-1 in a solid performance despite several key defencemen being scratched. Logan Stanley and Luke Schenn were held out to remain he...

item.title: Via Antonio commends Coco Martin's performance in 'Batang Quiapo'
item.description: In a recent statement, Via Antonio expressed admiration for Coco Martin, the lead actor of the popular TV series 'Batang Quiapo.' Antonio highlighted Martin's dedication, professionalism, and ability ...

item.title: South Africa Faces Impact of Escalating Middle East Crisis
item.description: South Africa, though over 8 000 km from the Middle East, is already feeling the repercussions of the intensifying Israel–US–Iran conflict. The crisis began with a US–Israeli strike that killed Iran’s ...

item.title: Residents Given 90 Days to Vacate Land for North Mara Gold Mine Expansion
item.description: The Mara Regional Commissioner, Col Evans Mtambi, has directed residents who have received compensation for land to move within 90 days so that the Barrick North Mara Gold Mine can expand. During his ...

item.title: Zanzibar Sees Improvements in Water and Electricity Services Under Mwinyi
item.description: During the first 100 days of President Hussein Ali Mwinyi's second term, Zanzibar has seen notable progress in both water and electricity services. According to Minister Nadir Abdullatif Al-Wardy, the...

item.title: Analysis of U.S. Patriot Missile Inventory and Potential Shortages
item.description: The United States currently relies on the PAC-3 MSE (Patriot Advanced Capability-3 Missile with Enhanced Segment) as its primary interceptor for high-priority threats. Production of this missile began...

item.title: Three Drug Dealers in Cotabato Linked to Former Terror Group Arrested
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Three individuals operating a drug den in Barangay Bagua 3, Cotabato City, have been arrested and are now facing legal charges for selling shabu and marijuana. The suspects, identified...

item.title: Greens’ Post-Election Review Highlights Lessons from 2025 Federal Poll
item.description: Following the 2025 Australian federal election, the Greens faced unexpected setbacks, losing three lower house seats including their safest seat, Melbourne. Despite receiving a primary vote similar to...

item.title: Tate McRae and Jack Hughes Are in an Exclusive Relationship
item.description: Pop singer Tate McRae, 22, and Team USA hockey player Jack Hughes, 24, have reportedly started dating exclusively, according to a source cited by Us Weekly. The couple was first spotted together in Ne...

item.title: First Lady Liza Marcos describes new Kamuning Footbridge as safer and more accessible
item.description: First Lady Liza Araneta-Marcos shared her experience climbing the former 'Mt. Kamuning,' a nickname for the old Kamuning footbridge known for its steep and long steps. She said the climb felt more lik...

item.title: President Marcos assures preparations for expected major fuel price increases and directs immediate fuel subsidy release
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. addressed concerns regarding impending significant increases in fuel prices at the pump, expected in the coming week due to ongoing tensions in the Middle Eas...

item.title: Roman Water Systems and Public Hygiene: How Infrastructure Sustained the Empire
item.description: Long before modern plumbing, ancient Rome had developed an advanced water and sanitation system that set it apart from most of the world. Starting in 312 B.C. with the construction of the Aqua Appia, ...

item.title: Carney Prioritizes Diversifying Canada’s Foreign Relations Amid Shifting Global Landscape
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's foreign policy approach has become increasingly pragmatic and transactional, focusing primarily on economic growth and reducing dependence on the United States. This appro...

item.title: Opposition questions Centre on Russian oil waiver amid Iran-Israel tensions
item.description: The opposition parties have raised serious concerns over the recent decision of the Indian government to grant a waiver on Russian oil imports. They have questioned whether such decisions are being ta...

item.title: Positive Update on Marsupial Populations
item.description: Recent reports have highlighted encouraging news regarding marsupial species, long regarded as vulnerable or at risk. Scientists and environmentalists have observed that certain marsupial populations ...

item.title: Western Cape re-enlists 62 experienced detectives to strengthen investigative capacity
item.description: Facing rising crime levels and stretched resources, the Western Cape has taken a proactive step by re-enlisting 62 former senior detectives who had previously retired or resigned from the South Africa...
